live_call_layer.stack: Matrix-compatible voice/video through the CallChat homeserver, local MatrixRTC authorization, and a self-hosted LiveKit SFU
live_call_layer.transport_security: WebRTC DTLS-SRTP plus Matrix device/account identity controls

q_call_layer.scope: Owner-controlled MatrixRTC, Matrix device identity, DTLS-SRTP media, TURN fallback, Shield status, and a PQC-ready research roadmap
q_call_layer.user_experience: Users start calls in the hosted CallChat Element client, which now prefers the local Element Call widget and owner-controlled MatrixRTC backend.

ionq_research_hook.purpose: Future approved quantum-assisted experiments, call policy attestation, or research evidence. Not required for ordinary calls.

claims_boundary: CallChat does not claim live audio packets are quantum encrypted today.
Q-Calls must be described as Matrix/WebRTC secure calls with a PQC-ready and optional IonQ research layer.
No IonQ API keys, ZMath private code, Shield secrets, Matrix signing keys, or recovery material are published here.
